description: Pure-Dart internet connectivity checker. Distinguishes "interface up" from "can reach the public internet". Pluggable probes, policies, and triggers.
rules: Answers "can I reach the public internet right now?" — not "is an interface up?". Don't treat OS connectivity (or connectivity_plus) as reachability; feed it through externalRecheckTrigger to trigger a recheck, not as source of truth.
InternetStatus is a sealed type — pattern-match with a switch on Reachable(:final quality) and Unreachable(:final failedProbes). Never assume a plain online/offline boolean.
Create a checker with InternetConnection(...), run a single check via `await checker.checkOnce()`, and always `await checker.dispose()` when finished to release timers and the stream.

Customize via pluggable seams: implement ConnectivityProbe (or HttpProbe with a ProbeTarget), choose a ReachabilityPolicy (AnyReachablePolicy or AllReachablePolicy), and pass slowThreshold to flag slow-but-reachable links via ConnectionQuality.
The package is pure Dart with a single `http` dependency — it runs in CLI, server, web, and Flutter contexts and has no Flutter dependency of its own.
